children.heading: Rules required
children.label: 1 Rules required
children.text: The Commission shall, in accordance with the requirements of this section, prescribe rules establishing procedures for the correction of billing errors with respect to telephone-billed purchases. The rules prescribed by the Commission shall also include provisions to prohibit unfair or deceptive acts or practices that evade such rules or undermine the rights provided to customers under this subchapter.

children.heading: Substantial similarity to credit billing
children.label: 2 Substantial similarity to credit billing
children.text: The Commission shall promulgate rules under this section that impose requirements that are substantially similar to the requirements imposed, with respect to the resolution of credit disputes, under the Truth in Lending and Fair Credit Billing Acts [ et seq., 1666 et seq.].15 U.S.C. 1601

children.heading: Treatment of rule
children.label: 3 Treatment of rule
children.text: A rule issued under paragraph (1) shall be treated as a rule issued under .section 57a(a)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Rulemaking schedule and procedure
children.label: b Rulemaking schedule and procedure
children.text: The Commission shall prescribe the rules under subsection (a) within 270 days after . Such rules shall be prescribed in accordance with .October 28, 1992section 553 of title 5

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: c Enforcement
children.text: Any violation of any rule prescribed under subsection (a) shall be treated as a violation of a rule under regarding unfair or deceptive acts or practices. Notwithstanding , communications common carriers shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the Commission for purposes of this subchapter.section 45 of this titlesection 45(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Correction of billing errors and correction of credit reports
children.label: d Correction of billing errors and correction of credit reports

children.text: The initiation of a billing review by a customer.
Responses by billing entities and providing carriers to the initiation of a billing review.
Investigations concerning delivery of telephone-billed purchases.
Limitations upon providing carrier responsibilities, including limitations on a carrier’s responsibility to verify delivery of audio information or entertainment.
Requirements on actions by billing entities to set aside charges from a customer’s billing statement.
Limitations on collection actions by billing entities and vendors.
The regulation of credit reports on billing disputes.
The prompt notification of credit to an account.
Rights of customers and telephone common carriers regarding claims and defenses.

The extent to which the regulations should diverge from requirements under the Truth in Lending and Fair Credit Billing Acts [ et seq., 1666 et seq.] in order to protect customers, and in order to be cost effective to billing entities.15 U.S.C. 1601
